title: Format event buffer 'Oldest' age as human-readable duration (hours+minutes)
analysis: The recon.html template (line 16) currently uses inline Jinja2 arithmetic `{{ (age // 60)|int }}m {{ (age % 60)|int }}s` to display the oldest event age. This always shows minutes+seconds regardless of magnitude, producing unreadable output like '1045m 36s' for 17+ hour old events. The fix adds a new `format_duration` Jinja2 filter in app.py that accepts seconds and returns a tiered human-readable string: under 60s shows seconds only ('45s'), 60s-3599s shows minutes+seconds ('10m 0s'), and 3600s+ shows hours+minutes ('17h 25m'). This is distinct from the existing `format_duration_ms` filter which takes milliseconds and returns a single-unit string without remainders. The template line is then simplified to `{{ age | format_duration }}`. The existing MOCK_BUFFER_STATS uses 600s (10 minutes), which stays in the minutes+seconds tier, so existing integration tests remain valid.

steps.description: Write unit tests for format_duration filter: test under-60s returns 'Xs', 60-3599s returns 'Xm Ys', 3600+ returns 'Xh Ym', edge cases (0s, exactly 60s, exactly 3600s), and filter registration on Jinja2 env
Implement format_duration function in app.py: accepts int/float seconds, returns tiered human-readable string. Register as Jinja2 filter on templates.env
Write integration test for buffer age display with large value (e.g. oldest_event_age_seconds=62736 → '17h 25m' appears in rendered HTML) and a test with small value (600s → '10m 0s')
Update recon.html line 16: replace inline `{{ (age // 60)|int }}m {{ (age % 60)|int }}s` with `{{ age | format_duration }}`

design_decisions.decision: Create a new format_duration filter instead of reusing format_duration_ms
design_decisions.rationale: format_duration_ms takes milliseconds and returns single-unit strings ('45s', '5m', '2.0h') without remainder components. The event buffer age needs compound output ('17h 25m', '10m 0s') from a seconds input. A new filter is cleaner than adapting the existing one, which would break the performance panel.

Under 60s, showing minutes is pointless ('0m 5s'). Between 1-59 minutes, seconds precision is useful. Over 60 minutes, seconds precision is noise — hours+minutes is sufficient for buffer staleness monitoring.

design_decisions.decision: Use a Jinja2 filter rather than fixing inline template math
design_decisions.rationale: Follows the existing project pattern (timeago, format_trigger, format_duration_ms are all registered filters). Centralizes formatting logic in testable Python code rather than untestable template logic.

reuse.how: Follow the same pattern: define a function, then register with templates.env.filters['name'] = fn
Add a TestFormatDuration class following the same pattern: import function, test edge cases, test filter registration

reuse.what: Integration test with mock buffer stats
reuse.how: Use _patch_recon_data(buffer_stats=...) to inject specific oldest_event_age_seconds values and assert the formatted string appears in HTML
